title: Famulus Blueprint Schema Annotation Protocol
description: Metadata required on canonical typed blueprint schemas so schema files define authoring, hashing, and enforcement links.

schema-shape.description: Validate required fields, values, and unknown-field rejection through the selected JSON Schema.
schema-shape.creation: Render each typed document from its concrete schema and reject values outside that schema before graph construction.
schema-shape.template: Render every root and sidecar from its concrete typed schema.

enforcement.note: JSON Schema is the current acceptance component.

canonical-id.description: Validate skill, interface, and behavior-source IDs and namespaces.
canonical-id.creation: Use the canonical skill, interface, or behavior-source namespace required by the concrete schema.
canonical-id.template: Use example-skill for local IDs and references.source.policy for the repository behavior-source example.

enforcement.note: The validator accepts isolated repository fixtures through validate(repo_root).

root-directory-id.description: Require a typed skill root ID to equal its skill directory name.
root-directory-id.creation: Name blueprint.yaml with the exact directory skill ID.

enforcement.note: load_skill_blueprint_graph enforces root-directory identity.

version-pin.description: Require positive versions and exact edge-to-target version matches.
version-pin.creation: Use positive versions and pin every declared edge to the target node version.

enforcement.note: The relationship validator checks reachable fixture versions.

file-binding.description: Require file-backed nodes to bind existing regular files and to keep child bindings out of local_hash_inputs.
file-binding.creation: Bind a file relative to the owning node root and list only additional node-local files in local_hash_inputs.
file-binding.template: Create SKILL.md, _rtx/_runner.py, _cx/_command, and references/policy.md before validating the example graph.

enforcement.note: Typed graph loading and source validation require existing regular bindings and validate declared local_hash_inputs.

root-interface-edge.description: Require one canonical root and an explicit default LLM interface edge.
root-interface-edge.creation: Declare the default LLM sidecar from the canonical root interfaces list.

enforcement.note: The typed skill schema is the current acceptance component.

relationship-edge.description: Resolve graph edges, target types, uniqueness, reachability, and cycles.
relationship-edge.creation: Declare only canonical, version-pinned graph edges with a target sidecar locator where required.
relationship-edge.template: The local root declares its four interface sidecars without duplicate edges.

enforcement.note: The relationship validator accepts the reachable local fixture.

access-control.description: Validate allow-all and caller allowlist behavior for cross-skill interface edges.
access-control.creation: Set allow_all_skills or enumerate the permitted caller skill IDs for cross-skill use.
access-control.template: The single-skill fixture has no cross-skill interface edge requiring an allowlist.

enforcement.note: The relationship validator enforces current access-control rules.

machine-binding-kind.description: Allow Python entrypoint files under _rtx and direct command files under _cx, never inline shell strings.
machine-binding-kind.creation: Use python-entrypoint for _rtx files and command-file for executable _cx files.
machine-binding-kind.template: The example has shared Python bindings and one command-file binding.

enforcement.note: The machine-interface schema selects the allowed binding forms.

dispatcher-argv-pattern.description: Validate dispatcher argument patterns without treating them as subsystem confinement.
dispatcher-argv-pattern.creation: Declare only caller-visible argv patterns and never use them as evidence of resource confinement.
dispatcher-argv-pattern.template: The example uses no optional argv pattern.

enforcement.note: The machine-interface schema validates declared pattern shape.

runtime-dependency.description: Validate declared runtime dependency applicability and required-interface platform support through the reachable interface closure.
runtime-dependency.creation: State interface support explicitly, list non-stdlib dependencies, use each dependency platform map to declare where each dependency applies, and never claim a platform absent from a required interface.
runtime-dependency.template: The example declares all platforms and no dependencies.

enforcement.note: The machine-interface schema validates dependency applicability-map shape, and the graph contract rejects platform claims not supported by required interfaces.

direct-io-description.description: Validate descriptive immediate IO while excluding it from certified contract hashes.
direct-io-description.creation: Describe immediate semantic IO without claiming subsystem confinement.
direct-io-description.template: The example uses empty reads, writes, and network lists.

enforcement.note: The source validator applies the typed direct_io schema and its descriptive, non-confinement contract.

filesystem-ownership.description: Validate machine and LLM file ownership and reader relationships.
filesystem-ownership.creation: Give each writable path one owner and enumerate any authorized readers.
filesystem-ownership.template: The example declares no owned filesystem paths.

enforcement.note: The source validator enforces typed filesystem ownership, reader authorization, and overlap rules.

behavior-source-edge.description: Require behavior-source edge targets, consumer-local reasons, and source-to-source edge typing.
behavior-source-edge.creation: Use a version-pinned behavior-source sidecar locator and state the consumer-local reason.
behavior-source-edge.template: The repository behavior-source sidecar is rendered as a schema-valid cross-root composition fixture.

enforcement.note: The relationship validator resolves version-pinned behavior-source edges, validates target types, and rejects duplicate or cyclic edges.

interface-body-use.description: Require typed LLM and behavior-source bodies to use declared canonical interface IDs instead of bare cross-skill names.
interface-body-use.creation: Name declared canonical interface IDs in bodies and never invoke a bare skill or direct _rtx/_cx path.
interface-body-use.template: The generated blocks are excluded from hand-authored body checks and the fixture body contains no undeclared reference.

enforcement.note: The dependency validator accepts isolated typed fixtures.

generated-contract-block.description: Require generated SKILL.md blueprint contract and interface blocks to match the canonical root and declared interfaces.
generated-contract-block.creation: Generate the contract block from the canonical root and generate the interface block from declared interfaces; do not hand-maintain either block.
generated-contract-block.template: template.yaml requires both generated SKILL.md blocks and the fixture writes both marker pairs.

enforcement.note: Blueprint and skill-body validators compare generated contract and interface blocks with the typed root and declared interfaces.

sidecar-naming.description: Derive hidden sidecar names deterministically from bound files, with a distinct qualifier when multiple nodes bind one file.
sidecar-naming.creation: For binding P, use dirname(P)/.basename(P).blueprint.yaml. For multiple bound nodes, use dirname(P)/.basename(P).<qualifier>.blueprint.yaml with a distinct qualifier for every node.
sidecar-naming.template: Use .SKILL.md.blueprint.yaml and the qualified _rtx/._runner.py.first.blueprint.yaml and _rtx/._runner.py.second.blueprint.yaml paths.

enforcement.note: Direct-owner loading enforces deterministic and qualified sidecar paths. Selected-provider loading derives same-binding peers from root locators and naming, so it applies the same rule without opening unused sidecars.

binding-tracked.description: Require every bound file and local hash input to be tracked by the relevant Git commit.
binding-tracked.creation: Create only repository-relative bindings and local_hash_inputs tracked by the captured commit; child-node bindings are not local inputs.
binding-tracked.template: The lifecycle fixture Git-adds SKILL.md, bindings, sidecars, and the repository policy source.

enforcement.note: Source validation requires authored sidecars, bindings, and local_hash_inputs to be present in the Git index.

binding-non-symlink.description: Require bindings and local hash inputs to be regular files rather than symlinks.
binding-non-symlink.creation: Resolve each path from the owning node root and reject symlink components, a symlink final path, and non-regular files; command bindings must also be executable under _cx.
binding-non-symlink.template: The lifecycle fixture writes regular files and makes _cx/_command executable.

enforcement.note: Graph and source validation reject symlink components, symlink endpoints, non-regular bindings, and invalid command-file placement or mode.

behavior-source-visibility.description: Restrict skill-local behavior sources to their declaring skill while making repository references visible to all skills.
behavior-source-visibility.creation: A node may reference its own skills/<skill>/ files or repository-root references/ files, never another skill's local behavior source; namespace ownership matches physical location.
behavior-source-visibility.template: template.yaml supplies references/.policy.md.blueprint.yaml as the repository-owned source example.

enforcement.note: Relationship validation permits declaring-skill local sources and repository references while rejecting direct edges to another skill's local source.

relationship-matrix.description: Allow only schema-declared edge types between typed blueprint node families.
relationship-matrix.creation: Use x-famulus.relationship_matrix as the complete source of allowed graph relations and target types.
relationship-matrix.template: The reachable fixture uses only edge and target-type combinations declared by the relationship matrix.

enforcement.note: The relationship validator loads the schema metadata matrix as the authoritative edge and target-type policy.

commit-backed-stamp.description: Require node and skill health records to carry one restorable Git source stamp for their local hash inputs.
commit-backed-stamp.creation: Set source.vcs to git, source.commit to the captured full Git object ID, and source.input_paths to sorted repository-relative local inputs that are tracked, regular, non-symlink, and byte-identical in HEAD, index, and worktree.
commit-backed-stamp.template: No author template emits health records; health.schema.json defines source only for node-health and skill-health records.

enforcement.note: Artifact-health production and admission require authenticated node-local source metadata tied to a restorable Git commit.

canonical-pooled-review.description: Keep pooled review derived, generated, and non-authoritative with respect to the canonical blueprint graph and node health.
canonical-pooled-review.creation: Render a pooled review only from one canonical root, reachable typed sidecars, and authenticated health records; never use it to declare graph edges or replace node health.
canonical-pooled-review.template: The template has no pooled-review input because pooled review is generated after canonical graph and health validation.

enforcement.note: Pooled review generation and health admission use the exact canonical graph and admitted node records without affecting canonical health.
